finding: The public deployment proof path required one credential-free runner that collects bounded DNS and HTTPS page identity, conditionally retrieves the served manifest, preserves manifest transport failures, and emits the composed exact-commit classification without overstating absence.
behavior: Collects the existing bounded public hostname probe.
Requests the well-known deployment manifest only after page identity is verified.
Preserves manifest HTTP, parse, and transport failures as explicit reasons.
Requires repository, surface, privacy flags, and exact source commit for successful classification.
Keeps public observation separate from Cloudflare account ownership and control-plane proof.
tests_committed: wrong public surface without manifest request

verification.source_readback: committed module and fixtures were read through the GitHub connector
verification.ci: combined status exposed only unrelated Vercel build-rate-limit failures; no successful runner test status was observed

limits: No actual Cloudflare hostname was available to execute the runner against.
No authenticated Cloudflare deployment or control-plane observation was performed.
Committed tests are not claimed passing without runtime evidence.
A successful public packet remains time-bounded and is not a diagnosis, treatment, payment, or conversion claim.
falsification_route: Run the committed Node test file in CI, then execute the runner against the actual Cloudflare hostname and expected deployed commit. Reject exact-commit proof if page identity, manifest transport, repository, surface, privacy flags, or source commit differ.
